claimText: The Taj Trapezium Zone (TTZ) encompasses three World Heritage Sites: the Taj Mahal, Agra Fort, and Fatehpur Sikri.
evidenceExcerpt: Coverage: Three World Heritage Sites- Taj Mahal, Agra Fort, and Fatehpur Sikri.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical and heritage coverage of the TTZ.
distractorHooks: Claiming it only covers the Taj Mahal
Including other nearby sites like Sikandra or Mathura temples

claimText: The Taj Trapezium Zone Pollution (Prevention and Control) Authority is established under the Environment Protection Act, 1986.
evidenceExcerpt: Taj Trapezium Zone Pollution (Prevention and Control) Authority: Under Environment Protection Act, 1986.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on the statutory backing of environmental authorities.
distractorHooks: Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981
Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974
Established by an executive order of the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

claimText: Industries within the Taj Trapezium Zone are categorized into Red, Green, Orange, and White by the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) based on their pollution potential.
evidenceExcerpt: Categories of Industries: Red, Green, Orange, and White based on pollution potential, as defined by the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB).
examUse: Statement-based question on industrial classification and regulatory frameworks in eco-sensitive zones.
distractorHooks: Categorized by the State Pollution Control Board (UPPCB)
Categorized based on capital investment or revenue rather than pollution potential
Categorized by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry

claimText: The Supreme Court directed the National Environmental Engineering Research Institute (NEERI) to assess the impact of the neighbouring glass industry on the Taj Mahal.
evidenceExcerpt: Supreme Court directed NEERI to assess the impact of neighbouring glass industry on Taj Mahal.
examUse: Statement-based question on the role of scientific institutions in environmental litigation and conservation.
distractorHooks: Directed the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) to assess the impact
Directed the National Green Tribunal (NGT) to assess the impact
Assessing the impact of the leather industry instead of the glass industry
